untranslatable_reason: Signal requires context-dependent logic based on market regime (BULL vs BEAR) and breakout direction (UP vs DOWN) to determine optimal lower shadow length. The performance differential (short shadows in bull/up scenarios vs tall shadows in other categories) cannot be cleanly expressed as single entry/exit rules without regime-aware conditional branching. Pattern identification and candle height thresholds relative to breakout price are not available as discrete indicators. Price target calculation via measure rule is author-silent and unmeasurable.
translation_notes: Attempted to capture lower shadow logic using lower_wick comparison to ATR as a proxy for shadow length classification. However, this signal fundamentally requires: (1) Market regime detection, (2) Prior breakout direction context, (3) Dynamic threshold adjustment based on candle height percentages (3.25%-5.31%), and (4) Conditional logic that varies by market context. The original source material provides performance statistics rather than mechanical entry/exit rules, making direct translation impossible without regime segmentation.
